functional.description: The system must expose a POST /documents endpoint to receive PDF uploads, validate file type and size (max 10MB), publish a DocumentUploadedEvent to a Kafka topic, and return 202 Accepted with a unique documentId.
functional.rationale: Core ingestion entry point. Asynchronous design decouples upload from processing via EDA.

functional.description: A Kafka consumer must process uploaded PDFs asynchronously: extract plain text, split into chunks, generate embeddings via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (abstracted via Spring AI Strategy Pattern), and persist chunks and vectors to PostgreSQL/pgvector.
functional.rationale: Core processing pipeline. Kafka decouples upload latency from CPU/IO-bound PDF processing.

functional.description: The system must expose a GET /documents/{id}/status endpoint returning the current processing status of a document: PROCESSING | READY | FAILED.
functional.rationale: Enables clients to poll processing progress without blocking the upload thread.

functional.description: The system must expose a POST /documents/{id}/query endpoint that accepts a natural language question, performs pgvector similarity search over the document's embeddings to retrieve top-K relevant chunks, builds a prompt with retrieved context, and returns a contextualized answer generated by GPT-4o-mini via Spring AI.
functional.rationale: Core RAG query interface — the primary value proposition of the Document Intelligence Service.

functional.description: The embedding provider (text-embedding-3-small) and LLM answer provider (gpt-4o-mini) must be abstracted via the Strategy Pattern using Spring AI abstractions, allowing future swap to other providers (Ollama, Cohere, etc.) without modifying core business logic.
functional.rationale: Prevents vendor lock-in and demonstrates clean architecture separation of concerns.

functional.description: The system must expose OpenAPI 3 documentation via Springdoc, accessible through Swagger UI, documenting all available endpoints with request/response schemas.
functional.rationale: Developer experience and testability. Primary interaction surface during development (Swagger UI, Postman).

functional.description: Failed Kafka messages (corrupted PDFs, processing errors) must be routed to a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Kafka topic after retry exhaustion for manual inspection.
functional.rationale: Operational resilience. Prevents silent message loss and enables post-mortem analysis.

nonFunctional.description: The Q&A endpoint (POST /documents/{id}/query) must respond within 5 seconds end-to-end under normal operating conditions.
nonFunctional.metric: p95 latency < 5000ms for the full RAG pipeline (embedding generation + pgvector search + LLM completion)

nonFunctional.description: Kafka consumers must be completely stateless, enabling horizontal scaling by adding consumer instances within the same consumer group without application code changes.
nonFunctional.metric: Zero shared mutable state in consumer service; no session-bound or instance-local data

nonFunctional.description: The PDF upload API must remain non-blocking and responsive during processing spikes. Upload and processing are fully decoupled via Kafka.
nonFunctional.metric: Upload endpoint p95 latency unaffected by consumer backpressure or queue depth

nonFunctional.description: The entire system stack (API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, Kafka) must be launchable via a single docker-compose up command for local development.
nonFunctional.metric: All services healthy and API accepting requests within 60 seconds of docker-compose up on standard developer hardware

constraints.description: Backend must be implemented in Java 21 with Spring Boot and Spring AI.
constraints.impact: Technology selection is fixed. All implementation patterns must conform to Spring Boot idioms and Spring AI abstractions.

constraints.description: Apache Kafka is the mandatory message broker for async document processing events. This is a deliberate architectural choice to demonstrate Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) competency.
constraints.impact: No in-process queues or synchronous processing. All document lifecycle events flow through Kafka topics.

constraints.description: PostgreSQL >= 15 with pgvector extension is the mandatory vector store and primary relational database.
constraints.impact: Schema migrations must enable the pgvector extension. Vector columns use pgvector native type.

constraints.description: Maximum PDF file size is 10MB. System is optimized for text-based PDFs to keep processing latency manageable.
constraints.impact: API must validate and reject oversized uploads with 413 Payload Too Large before any Kafka enqueue.

constraints.description: No authentication or authorization for this MVP. System operates in single-tenant mode with all endpoints publicly accessible.
constraints.impact: Auth hardening is explicitly out of scope. Security layer must be added before any production exposure.

constraints.description: No OCR processing. Only plain-text-extractable PDFs are supported in this MVP.
constraints.impact: Scanned PDFs, image-only PDFs, and password-protected PDFs will result in Document status FAILED.

constraints.description: Claim Check Pattern via shared Docker named volume. The API container writes the PDF to a named volume; the consumer container reads from the same volume using the filePath from the Kafka event. Both containers must mount this volume.
constraints.impact: Horizontal scaling of consumers is limited to a single Docker host. Multi-host scale-out requires migration to object storage (MinIO/S3). Acceptable for MVP scope. storageRoot path must be externalized via environment variable (PDF_STORAGE_PATH).

assumptions.description: All uploaded PDFs contain machine-readable, extractable text. Scanned or image-based PDFs are explicitly out of scope and will fail processing.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A significant portion of real-world PDFs (contracts, invoices, scans) would silently fail, degrading the service's practical utility.

assumptions.description: Each Q&A query is stateless — no conversation history or multi-turn context is maintained between separate query requests.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Users expecting chat-like follow-up questions would receive disconnected, context-blind answers.

assumptions.description: System operates in single-tenant mode with no user identity, ownership model, or access control on documents.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Any multi-user scenario would expose all documents to all callers — a critical security gap requiring auth before production use.

assumptions.description: The shared Docker volume provides sufficient disk space to hold concurrent in-flight PDFs (uploads awaiting consumer processing). With 10MB max file size and typical processing time of seconds, concurrent load is bounded and negligible.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Under extreme concurrency or consumer lag, disk space could be exhausted before cleanup. Mitigated by 10MB file size limit and consumer file deletion post-processing.

flow: Validate file type (must be application/pdf) and size (max 10MB)
Create Document record with status=PROCESSING
Save PDF bytes to shared Docker volume at path: {storageRoot}/{documentId}/{filename}
Persist storagePath to Document record
Publish DocumentUploadedEvent { documentId, filePath } to Kafka topic (Claim Check Pattern — lightweight event, no PDF bytes)
Return 202 Accepted with { documentId }

states.loading: N/A — response is immediate; processing is async via Kafka consumer
states.error: 413 if file exceeds 10MB | 400 if not a PDF | 503 if Kafka is unavailable | 500 if volume write fails

flow: Fetch Document entity by ID from PostgreSQL
Return { documentId, status, filename } — status is PROCESSING | READY | FAILED

states.error: 404 Not Found if documentId does not exist

flow: Verify Document exists and status=READY (else 404 or 409)
Generate embedding for the question via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (Spring AI)
Execute pgvector cosine similarity search to retrieve top-K most relevant Chunks
Assemble prompt: system instructions + retrieved context chunks + user question
Call GPT-4o-mini via Spring AI to generate natural language answer
Return { answer, sourceChunks } to client

states.loading: N/A — synchronous request; total expected latency < 5s
states.error: 404 if document not found | 409 if status != READY | 502 if OpenAI API unavailable

interactions.trigger: Kafka Consumer receives DocumentUploadedEvent { documentId, filePath }
flow: Read filePath from event payload (Claim Check Pattern — file already on shared volume)
Open and read PDF bytes from shared volume at filePath
Extract plain text from PDF using PDF parsing library
Split text into overlapping chunks: 1000 chars per chunk, 200 char overlap (configurable via app.rag.chunkSize / app.rag.chunkOverlap)
Generate embedding vector per chunk via OpenAI Strategy (Spring AI)
Persist Chunk + Embedding records to PostgreSQL in a single transaction
Update Document status to READY
Delete PDF file from shared volume (claim check cleanup — prevent unbounded disk growth)
On any failure: update Document status to FAILED, publish failed event to DLQ, leave file for inspection

states.loading: Document status remains PROCESSING during consumer execution
states.error: Status set to FAILED; event sent to DLQ for manual inspection; error logged; file NOT deleted on failure

edgeCases.scenario: Kafka broker is offline when client attempts PDF upload
edgeCases.handling: Fail fast — return 503 Service Unavailable immediately. Do not persist Document record. Client must retry.

edgeCases.scenario: PDF is corrupted, password-protected, or image-only (no extractable text)
edgeCases.handling: Kafka consumer catches parsing exception, updates Document status to FAILED, logs structured error, publishes event to DLQ.

edgeCases.scenario: OpenAI API is unavailable when client submits a Q&A query
edgeCases.handling: Return 502 Bad Gateway with descriptive error. No automatic retry — client is responsible for retry logic.

edgeCases.scenario: Client queries a document that still has status=PROCESSING
edgeCases.handling: Return 409 Conflict with message: 'Document is still being processed. Please retry after polling /status.'
Return 413 Payload Too Large before any Kafka enqueue or DB write. Validation at API boundary.

edgeCases.scenario: Kafka consumer fails repeatedly on the same message (poison pill scenario)
edgeCases.handling: After configured max retry exhaustion, route message to Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) topic. Alert via log. No infinite retry loop.

edgeCases.scenario: Consumer receives a DocumentUploadedEvent but the PDF file is missing from the shared volume (race condition, volume unmount, or disk failure)
edgeCases.handling: Consumer catches FileNotFoundException, updates Document status to FAILED, logs structured error with filePath, publishes event to DLQ. File cleanup is skipped (nothing to delete).

terminology.definition: A PDF file uploaded to the system, tracked as a domain entity with a processing lifecycle status (PROCESSING → READY | FAILED).
A discrete, fixed-size fragment of text extracted from a Document, sized for optimal embedding generation and semantic retrieval.
A high-dimensional numerical vector representing the semantic meaning of a Chunk, generated by OpenAI text-embedding-3-small via the Spring AI abstraction.
A natural language question submitted by the client to the Q&A endpoint. Stateless — not persisted in the database.
The set of top-K Chunks retrieved via pgvector similarity search that are most semantically relevant to the user's Query, used to ground the LLM's answer.
Dead Letter Queue — a dedicated Kafka topic where messages are routed after exhausting retry attempts, enabling manual inspection and replay.
Event-Driven Architecture — the architectural pattern governing PDF ingestion, where DocumentUploadedEvent decouples the upload API from async consumer processing.

Enterprise Integration Pattern where large payloads (PDFs) are stored externally (shared volume) and only a lightweight reference (documentId + filePath) is passed through the message broker (Kafka). Prevents Kafka message size limitations and broker memory pressure.

The root directory on the shared Docker volume where uploaded PDFs are temporarily stored. Configured via PDF_STORAGE_PATH environment variable. Files are organized as {storageRoot}/{documentId}/{filename}.

openQuestions.question: How should raw PDF bytes be stored and referenced in the Kafka event?
openQuestions.resolution: Claim Check Pattern. API saves PDF to shared Docker named volume at {storageRoot}/{documentId}/{filename}. Kafka event carries only { documentId, filePath }. Consumer reads file from volume, then deletes it after successful processing. See ADR-001.

openQuestions.question: What chunk size and overlap strategy for text splitting?
openQuestions.resolution: 1000 characters per chunk, 200 character overlap. Configurable via app.rag.chunkSize and app.rag.chunkOverlap in application.properties. Rationale: balances embedding API call count, context coherence, and storage cost for text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions).

openQuestions.question: What is K for pgvector similarity search? Configurable?
openQuestions.resolution: K=5 as default. Configurable via app.rag.topK in application.properties. Rationale: K=5 provides sufficient context for focused Q&A without diluting the LLM prompt. Users can tune upward (K=10) for complex, multi-topic documents.
